_publications._publicationTitle: Patient-Derived Tumor Organoid Rings for Histologic Characterization and High-Throughput Screening

_contextSnippet: Mini-rings in 96-well plates enable automated high-throughput drug screening while maxi-rings in 24-well plates support histologic characterization and downstream biochemical analysis
details: Two plate formats are described: mini-rings in 96-well plates (5,000–10,000 cells/well) for automated high-throughput drug screening, and maxi-rings in 24-well plates (~100,000 cells/well) for histologic characterization and downstream biochemical analysis
